Question Text: Think about the last time you needed a performance overview across all your accounts or locations. What did you actually do?
Question Context / Instructions: Be honest — we're mapping real workflows, not ideal ones. Select all that apply.
Answer Options: Logged into each account one at a time to pull numbers; Exported CSVs from multiple accounts, then combined them in a spreadsheet; Asked someone else (team member, agency, vendor) to pull and compile the data; Used a dashboard or BI tool that consolidates some data automatically; Gave up or skipped it because it was too time-consuming; Other (please describe)

Question Text: In a typical month, approximately how much time does your team spend assembling, formatting, or reconciling reports across multiple accounts?
Question Context / Instructions: Include time spent by anyone involved — you, coordinators, analysts, agencies.
Answer Options: Less than 1 hour; 1-4 hours; 5-10 hours; 11-20 hours; 20+ hours
Answer Descriptions: Less than 1 hour per month; 1-4 hours per month; 5-10 hours per month (About 1-2 hours per week); 11-20 hours per month (About half a day per week); More than 20 hours per month (Essentially a part-time job)

Question Text: Which of these problems have directly affected your work or decisions in the last 90 days?
Question Context / Instructions: Only select items you've personally experienced — not theoretical concerns. Select all that apply.
Answer Options: No single view across accounts; Could not drill down from totals to details; Data was stale by the time reports were ready; Manual assembly introduced errors or inconsistencies; Could not compare locations or teams side by side; Executives or stakeholders asked for data you could not quickly provide; Could not share reports without manual formatting or rework; Other (please describe)
Answer Descriptions: Had to piece together data from multiple logins or exports; Saw an aggregate number but couldn't identify which location or campaign drove it; Reports were outdated before you could act on them; Mismatched date ranges, duplicate data, or formula mistakes in combined spreadsheets; Needed to benchmark performance but had no easy way to do it; Scrambled to pull numbers for a meeting, review, or board request; Had to reformat exports before they were presentable;

Question Text: Describe a specific moment when the lack of consolidated reporting caused you to miss something, delay a decision, or present incomplete data.
Question Context / Instructions: Help us understand the real cost. Example: 'We couldn't identify an underperforming location until the quarterly review because no one was looking at all 12 accounts together.' Example: 'I spent 3 hours pulling CSVs for a board meeting and still had mismatched date ranges.'

Question Text: If you had a single dashboard across all your accounts, which metrics would you check first?
Question Context / Instructions: Select your top 3 — this forces a real tradeoff so we know what matters most.
Answer Options: Engagement rates (open rate, click-through rate) by location or team; Deliverability and list health across all accounts; Campaign performance comparisons across locations; Revenue or ROI tied to email activity; Subscriber growth and churn by account; Team or location activity and sending volume; Trend lines over time (week-over-week, month-over-month); Other (please describe)
Answer Descriptions: Open rate, click-through rate broken down per account; Bounce rates, spam complaints, deliverability trends; Same campaign across different locations — who's outperforming?; Attributable revenue, conversion rates, downstream impact; Net new subscribers, unsubscribe trends, list size trajectories; Who's actively sending? Who's gone quiet?; Are things getting better or worse, week-over-week and month-over-month?;

Question Text: How fresh does your cross-account data need to be for it to actually change what you do?
Question Context / Instructions: This helps us decide between real-time infrastructure vs. periodic updates.
Answer Options: Real-time (within minutes); Same-day (refreshed every few hours); Next-day (updated overnight); Weekly summary is fine
Answer Descriptions: I make daily send/pause/adjust decisions based on live data; I check data daily but don't need it to the minute; Yesterday's data is fast enough for my decisions; I review performance in weekly or biweekly cycles

Question Text: If a competing tool offered consolidated cross-account reporting today, how would that affect your decision to stay with your current platform?
Question Context / Instructions: Be candid — this directly shapes how we prioritize this investment.
Answer Options: Would actively evaluate switching; Would factor it into next renewal decision; Nice to have but would not switch; Not a factor in platform decisions
Answer Descriptions: Cross-account reporting is important enough to change platforms for; It wouldn't trigger an immediate switch, but would weigh heavily at renewal; I'd appreciate it, but other factors matter more; Reporting isn't part of my tool evaluation criteria

Question Text: If we built this — a single dashboard showing all your accounts with drill-down to any location, team, or campaign — what would you do with it first?
Question Context / Instructions: Paint us a picture of the first 5 minutes. Example: 'I'd finally be able to show our VP which 3 locations are dragging down our overall click rate.' Example: 'I'd compare our Q1 welcome series across all 20 franchisees to find out why some convert at 2x the rate.'
